rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ALICIA VENENCIANO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/22/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain why the conservator was paid $2,030.00 in fees during this account period for the 3rd account fees when the April 7, 2021 order says page 2 lines 11-13 that conservator is allowed $2,030.00 as just a reasonable compensation and is authorized to pay herself the $430.00 balance due after having taken fees on account totaling $1,600. It appears the conservator needs to pay back the conservatorship and file a receipt. Please explain whether the conservatorship is entitled to interest. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/07/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BEVERLY QUAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although there were no schedules to support the statutory fee calculation, as required by CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6), the attorney's costs were not itemized (set forth in detail to show what was filed and when), the petition caption did not disclose all relief sought, as required by CRC 7.102, and the verification was made the administrator of the estate of Kwan Quan, not this estate's administrator. The decedent died intestate, not testate. Notice must also be given to the Director of Health Care Services, if the decedent was the surviving spouse of a person who received Medi-Cal benefits. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution". [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZETHER MCGRIGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/13/22. (No earlier availability.) The real property was not appraised by the Probate Referee. [Probate Code Section 8902(b).] The I&A filed on 9/24/21 (not 9/9/21) -- more than four months after Letters were issued to the executor on 2/5/21 -- showed a total of $810,180, not $750,180, as alleged in paragraph 9 of the petition. The "approximate" amount of cash is also not sufficient. Item 5 of the I&A was also not completed. (This Court has no jurisdiction over property outside of this state.) If the Probate Referee cannot complete the appraisal of the real property in time for the executor to file her Corrected I&A and a second amended petition (or an amendment to her amended petition) before the continued hearing date in order to give proper notice of the second amended petition (or amendment to the amended petition), the hearing will have to be continued again. The creditor claims listed in paragraph 6 of the petition are not correct. The register of actions reflects three claims filed (on 10/20/20, 2/24/21, and 4/1/21) and, other than the $219 claim, for amounts other than those stated in this petition. Paragraphs 7 and 13 of the petition have optional allegations. Which allegation is true? Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. Detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale must be filed to support the statutory fee. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] Under what authority did the executor distribute $20,000 to Wilhelmina McGriger? A general pecuniary devise not made within one year from the date of death bears interest. [Probate Code Section 12003 and 12001.] The "supplement" file stamped on 10/12/22 but not actually e-filed until 10/17/22 (when it was first viewable to the public) AMENDED the petition, which required notice. [CRC 7.3 and 7.53(a).] Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution". [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/2/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELWOOD GEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom use the Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 and Password: 514879 ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Special Letters of Administration expire 10/25/2022. Petitioner has never received Special Letters as the first attempt at obtaining letters was made 10/19/2022 but was rejected for failing to match the order. It appears there has been no attempt to address any of the Examiner notes for this petition. This was the same issue at the 12/14/2021 hearing and the petition was taken off calendar after no one appeared. 2) Examiner Notes: a. A new proof of publication that indicates a lost will is requested to be admitted must be published and filed [See LRSF 14.20.] b. 15 days' notice must be given on the mandatory judicial council form DE-121 indicating lost will and filed with the court and include the required Local Rule 14.1 D. language [See LRSF 14.20.] c. The filed DE-147 does not include the petitioner's phone number as required on page 2 under #2. d. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [See LRSF 14.18 E.] e. A copy of decedent's lost will must be lodged with the court as if it were an original. Attorney Peter Kutrubes lodged his client Shu Qing Gee's Will with the court instead of Decedent's Will. Shu Qing Gee's Will was removed from this case and put into a lodged Will file as the attorney Peter Kutrubes made no attempt to correct this error. f. A hard copy of the proposed order must be provided to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [See LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. g. The petition caption is incomplete as counsel did not select a box regarding IAEA authority. h. The petition on page 3 item 5. A. (3) or (3) must be answered. i. If a will names a trust or trustee as a devisee, the trustee(s) must be clearly identified in item 8 of the petition. Where the personal representative and the sole trustee are the same person, or if there is no trustee, the beneficiaries of the trust must be clearly identified in item 8 of the petition. Probate Code 1208(b). [See LRSF 14.18 C. 2)]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE AKIKO SPINDLE 2021 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/14/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: While the petition was file stamped 9/7/22, due to a paucity of court clerks, it was not actually e-filed until 9/13/22, when it was first available for viewing. In any event, notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the petition requiring notice: here, the proof of service indicates that notice was mailed on 9/6/22, before the petition was even uploaded for e-filing. Proof of service of notice of the hearing on 12/14/22 must be filed in time to deliver a courtesy copy to the assigned examiner by 12/2/22. Where a trust is related to a conservatorship, the related case number must be noted in parentheses under the pleading caption in the related matter. [LRSF 14.47 B.3.] No courtesy copy of the financial account statements has been received by the assigned examiner. It does not appear that the trustee or her attorney has made the necessary declaration regarding whether or not the statements are originals. [See LRSF 14.34 D and also CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts.] The minutes from the 12/7/21 hearing in the conservatorship case indicate that the Court directed the first trust accounting to be for the period beginning 6/10/21, when the trust was created, and ending 6/30/22. The minutes for the 8/22/22 hearing in this case only reflect that Counsel claimed that the accounting should be filed by 8/29/22. In any event this accounting, beginning on 12/1/21, shows assets that differ in both the number of units and values of each holding from the assets shown in the conservator's 3rd account ending on 11/30/21. There are also different account numbers. (And assets are also presented in a different order from the conservator's third accounting Schedule E, which makes reviewing the assets difficult.) Paragraph 20 of the petition appears to be incomplete. Were tax returns filed or just prepared? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/2/22. If the accounting is amended and there is insufficient time to give 30 days' notice of the amended accounting petition after it is e-filed, Counsel should contact the assigned examiner to request an additional continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JACK JAY CARLSON III
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/30/22. The proposed personal representative still has not filed anything to confirm that the decedent's mother expressly consented to electronic service of notice. [CRC 2.251(b).] A courtesy copy of either her express written consent to electronic service or proof of service of notice of the hearing on 11/30/22 having been mailed to her at least 15 days before that date must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 11/18/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TONY ALAN HOLLIFIELD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/06/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Must file the original will. (See Probate Code 8200 et seq.) 2) Publication must be re-done for the Second Amended Petition. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. (See CRC 7.53). 3) Notice of the Second Amended Petition was given prior to the petition being filed and 15 days notice must be re-done and include the required language found is Local Rule 14.1 D. 6) 4) Please explain the relation of Erin Pursue and Lamona Wood to Decedent. (See petition page 4 #8). A file stamped single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/21/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARTHUR STOCKTON HOUGH JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Declaration filed on 10/11/22 states that two distant relatives of Decedent's father were identified and given notice, but their names were not given in the declaration, and the information required in Item 8 of the probate petition (age, relationship, and address) were not provided. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JODY LAMAR WILSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The decedent held title to two cars and one financial account in his sole name. The petitioner/surviving spouse alleges that all the assets sought to be determined and confirmed as community property were acquired during marriage with community funds. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL GREGORY MITCHELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN WISHNEFSKY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/22/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitioner is requesting to admit decedents will dated 6/18/2019. The court has 2 wills 1 is dated 6/18/2019 and the other will is dated 6/20/2019 and the 6/20/2019 will appears to revoke the prior will that Petitioner is seeking to admit. 2) Marc Geller is first named executor and has not filed a declination to serve. 3) Weber Hal and Irene Jaffe's date of death is not specified. The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D. 1)] 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 5) 15 days notice on DE-121 must be re-done and include the required language in Local Rule 14.1 D. 6) 6) A hard copy of the proposed order must be provided to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. A file stamped single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/7/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HENRY NANG TOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the proposed administrator to orally provide missing or incorrect information regarding her petition. 1) Item 7 of the petition still has not been addressed, as noted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 8/30/22. 2) Item 6f of the petition is not the first box that applies if the decedent's sibling survived. If, however, the decedent had a predeceased spouse who had an interest in real or personal property that passed to the decedent, the hearing might need to be continued further for the heirs of that predeceased spouse to be identified and to be given notice. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/13/22. No response by the deadline of 10/14/22 (or even as of 10/17/22) to the many issues posted in the tentative ruling for the initial hearing on 8/30/22. If issues are not addressed in time to deliver the proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and all required supporting papers to the assigned examiner by 12/2/22, an appearance will be required on 12/13/22 for the Court to determine if the petition should be taken off calendar as a waste of the Court's time and resources. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
